title: GENERAL STANDARD FOR THE LABELLING OF NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS OF FOODS CXS 346-2021

sections.section: GENERAL STANDARD FOR THE LABELLING OF NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS OF FOODS CXS 346-2021
sections.summary: Title only: the document is the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Adoption date only: this standard was adopted in 2021.
Purpose: to facilitate harmonized labelling of non-retail containers of food and to specify what information must appear on labels and what information may be provided by other means.

Scope: the Standard applies to labelling of non-retail containers of food (excluding food additives and processing aids) not intended for direct sale to consumers, including information provided in accompanying documents or by other means and its presentation.

Definitions: relevant terms from the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) apply, and this Standard defines 'food business' (entities across production to distribution) and 'non-retail container' (containers not intended for direct sale, whose contents are for further food business activities).

General principles: the principles of CXS 1-1985 apply as appropriate; labelling for non-retail containers must be distinct from prepackaged food labelling, containers must be clearly identifiable as non-retail, non-retail status is determined by the selling/distributing food business, requirements should consider business/authority capabilities, information may be provided by means other than a label when allowed by the competent authority, and all information must be traceable to the food in the container.

sections.section: ## 5. MANDATORY INFORMATION REQUIREMENTS ON THE LABEL
sections.summary: Heading only: introduces the mandatory information requirements that shall appear on labels of non-retail containers (details provided in subsequent subsections).
Lot identification: each non-retail container must be marked (in code or otherwise) to clearly identify the producing factory and the lot(s) of the food.
Date marking and storage instructions must be provided when required for the safety or integrity of the product.

Identification of a non-retail container: non-retail containers must be clearly identifiable as such, and if not obvious they must bear a statement (e.g., 'NON-RETAIL CONTAINER' or 'NOT FOR DIRECT SALE TO CONSUMER') or other marking indicating they are not for direct sale.
Name and address: the name and address of the manufacturer, packer, distributor, importer, exporter or vendor of the food shall be declared on the non-retail container.

sections.section: ## 6. MANDATORY INFORMATION REQUIREMENTS BY MEANS OTHER THAN ON THE LABEL
sections.summary: Mandatory info by other means: information required under Section 5 (and additional details to enable safe preparation and prepackaged labelling, plus net contents) may be provided in accompanying documents or other means but must be traceable to the food; if all required information is on the label, these provisions do not apply.

sections.section: ## 7. PROVISIONS FOR SPECIFIC TYPES OF NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS
sections.summary: Heading only: introduces provisions for specific types of non-retail containers.

sections.section: ## 7.1 Non-retail container used as food transportation unit 13
sections.summary: Non-retail containers used as food transportation units that cannot bear a label must have all information required by Section 5 and Subsection 6.1 provided in accompanying documents or by other appropriate means (e.g., electronically) and effectively traceable to the food.

sections.section: 7.2 Non-retail container containing multiple types of food
sections.summary: When a non-retail container contains multiple types of food, the mandatory information required by Section 5 and Subsection 6.1 must be provided for all food types contained therein.

Non-retail containers that provide visual and legible access to all information required by Section 5 for the prepackaged foods inside are exempted from carrying that Section 5 information on the container.
Heading only for Section 8 on presentation of information; no substantive provisions in this item.

General presentation requirements for non-retail containers: labels must remain affixed, mandatory and other required statements must be clear, prominent, readily legible and tamper-evident, mandatory Section 5 information must be in a prominent and readily accessible position under normal handling, and information provided other than on the label must be readily accessible, legible and clearly displayed.

sections.section: 8.1.1 Labels on non-retail containers of foods shall be applied in such a manner that they will not  become separated from the container.
sections.summary: Labels on non-retail food containers must be applied so they will not become separated from the container.

sections.section: 8.1.2 Information and the statements required to appear on the label by virtue of this Standard or any other Codex standards shall be clear, prominent, readily legible and applied in such a manner that any tampering with it will be evident.
sections.summary: Information and statements required by this Standard or other Codex standards must be clear, prominent, readily legible and applied so that any tampering will be evident.

sections.section: 8.1.3 The mandatory information required on the label under Section 5 shall appear in a prominent position on the non-retail container and shall be readily accessible under normal handling and use of the container.
sections.summary: Mandatory information required under Section 5 must appear in a prominent position on the non-retail container and be readily accessible under normal handling and use.

sections.section: 8.1.4 Information that is provided by means other than the label shall be readily accessible, legible and clearly displayed.
sections.summary: Information provided by means other than the label must be readily accessible, legible and clearly displayed.

If the original label language is not acceptable to the competent authority or food business in the country of sale, the mandatory information should be provided in the required language by re-labelling, a supplementary label, accompanying documents or other means, and that information must fully and accurately reflect the original label.

sections.section: 8.2.1 If the language on the original label is not acceptable to the competent authority or the food business in the country in which the product is sold, the mandatory information in the required language should be provided in the form of re-labelling, a supplementary label and/or in the accompanying documents or by means other than on the label to meet the requirements of the country in which the product is sold.
sections.summary: When the original label language is unacceptable to the competent authority or food business in the country of sale, mandatory information in the required language should be provided via re-labelling, a supplementary label, accompanying documents or other means to meet that country's requirements.

sections.section: 8.2.2 The mandatory information provided in the required  language shall fully  and accurately reflect that  of the original label.
sections.summary: Mandatory information provided in the required language must fully and accurately reflect the original label.
